_meta.method: Per research-handoff-non-english.md recovery pipeline: extract named instruments from search_languages.notes -> verify via real WebSearch/WebFetch -> dedup against evidence_sources -> ingest via migration. Empties/negative findings recorded, not dropped (S6).
_meta.scope_disclosure: Two passes, per handoff S7 honest-limit clause. Batch 1: Tier-0 registry backlog (6 refs), Tier-1 richest 2 slugs by non-EN yield (deaf-classroom-reverberation-time, stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility) + a partial pass on wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design (3 of ~15 named instruments), plus the 5-language Global-South investigation. Batch 2: completed wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design (10 more instruments, Portugal re-confirmed as genuine absence), filled the NL ramp gap and corrected the ES ramp-gradient citation for stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility, and re-attempted (unsuccessfully, tooling-blocked) direct-read verification of the 9 Global-South leads. The remaining Tier-1/Tier-2 slugs (luminance-contrast-and-pattern 74 non-EN hits, sensory-room-user-control 42, cognitive-wayfinding-design/deaf-spatial-design/mental-health-built-environment/accessible-circulation-geometry 19-23 each) and the full 14-language x 24-jurisdiction sweep remain a running fraction, not claimed complete.

tier0_registry_backlog.note: Deafblind communication modalities guide (DINF, 1998). Real-retrieval verified via WebSearch (primary URL 403'd to direct fetch). Linked to deafblind-built-environment-design (DBL-09).

BCA Code on Accessibility in the Built Environment 2025, Ch.8 Signage and Wayfinding. English-language (Singapore official language) -- adds JURISDICTIONAL not LANGUAGE diversity. Linked to wayfinding-global-south (WGS-11).
DIN 18041:2016 -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00329. No new row created.
Decree 241/2017 -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00424 and REF-00459 (door provisions). Relinked REF-00424 to stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility (SRB-19) this session.
Arrete du 20 avril 2017 + CEREMA BIM guidance -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00350.

GB 50763-2012 (accessible entrances) -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00462 (exact title match: "无障碍出入口"). Relinked REF-00462 to stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility (SRB-16) this session.

lang_detected_hygiene_fix.criterion: standard_number-bearing rows in a single-official-language jurisdiction, whose lang_detected disagreed with that jurisdiction's language and whose pub_title/standard_number carries unambiguous native-language text or a national-standard identifier pattern (DIN/TEK17/BFS/GB/NS/SFS/JIS/UNI/Loi/Arrete/Decree/DL/DM).

deliberately_not_corrected.reason: Publisher is Dutch, but content reads as an English-language post-occupancy-evaluation report; no native-language text in title itself. Left as lang_detected=en pending direct verification.
Registry note itself hedges "likely BAGSO... brochure"; no native-language text in title. Left uncorrected.
iGlobenews international news article -- genuinely English-language reporting on a Dutch facility, not a Dutch source.
Seoul municipal guideline commonly issued bilingually; no native-language text in title. Left uncorrected.
Danish institution but title reads as English gloss with only one embedded Danish word (institution context); insufficient evidence to override.

lang_detected_hygiene_fix.jurisdiction_hygiene_applied: INTL -> INT normalization (5 rows), per db/migrations/0001-jurisdiction-hygiene.sql item 1. Item 3 (19 null jurisdictions) intentionally left untouched -- would require guessing (forbidden by S3).

additions.note: Search-notes candidate NF S31-080:2006 was verified NOT to cover classrooms (offices/tertiary only); dropped and replaced with the correct citation.

still_unresolved: JA (no legally-binding national classroom-RT standard -- genuine absence, not a gap in search)
DA (no single clean classroom-RT standard identified; Arbejdstilsynet + BR18 + DCUM cluster, needs a dedicated pass)
KO/PT (search_languages notes explicitly mark these as NOT SEARCHED for this slug)

additions_batch2.note: Height-tiered ramp table (1:12/1:16/1:20 by 0.25m/0.5m breakpoints), confirmed accurate, HIGH confidence.

additions_batch2.standard: DB-SUA Seccion SUA 1 sec.4.3 Tabla 4.1 (not SUA 9 as originally claimed)
additions_batch2.note: The search-notes-claimed ramp table (12%/8%/6%/4%) was WRONG -- it conflated the real new-construction table (10%/8%/6% + 2% cross-fall, SUA 1 sec.4.3) with a different document's existing-building tolerances (DA DB-SUA/2: 12%/10%/8%/6%). Relinked with corrected figures; the wrong table is not cited anywhere.

still_unresolved: KO (장애인 편의증진법 ramp provisions named but not cross-checked)
DA DB-SUA/2 (Spain existing-building ramp tolerance table, distinct from the corrected DB-SUA new-construction table above -- identified but not verified/ingested)

additions_batch1.standard: Helsedirektoratet Nasjonal faglig retningslinje for demens

additions_batch2.standard: Bekendtgorelse om maerkning af demensegnede plejeboliger (BEK 2243/2020)
SBi-anvisning 259 (2nd ed. 2019, year corrected from claimed 2014)
Guia de Practica Clinica sobre Alzheimer (GPC 484, 2010)
additions_batch2.note: Granular color-therapy content originally attributed to this guide actually belongs to a different Spanish publication (Fundacion Pilares/AFAD) -- ingested for confirmed general environmental-modification content only.
Chosen over the alternative candidate (4th National Dementia Plan), which was confirmed real but policy-level only with no design content.

Verified to be primarily a directory/referral portal, not a technical design standard -- ingested at tier 3 (grey), cited only for confirmed home-safety-checklist and resident-group-size content, not the more granular corridor-geometry/mirror-avoidance claims in the original search notes.

wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design.still_unresolved: COMPLETE for clearly-named instruments (batch 1 + batch 2 = 13 of ~15 originally named). Portugal (PT) independently re-checked with 5 additional search queries this session -- GENUINE ABSENCE CONFIRMED, not ingested; one unconfirmed lead flagged (IGAS 2023 ERPI Referencial de Boas Praticas, content unread, 403-blocked). One companion document flagged but not chased: the Italian 'Linee guida del Ministero della Sanita n. 1' (~1994, exact date uncertain across sources) alongside REF-00746.

SW.reasoning: Tooling reachability ruled out (Swahili content is reachable for other topics). Kenya and Tanzania both have real, current accessibility-standards regimes (Tanzania Persons with Disabilities Act 2010; Kenya Persons with Disabilities Act + National Building Code 2024 referencing KS ISO 21542:2011) but BOTH are published only in English -- no Swahili-original or Swahili-translated accessibility building standard was found despite deliberately-localized queries.

ID.verdict: (a)/(b) tooling reachability / query construction -- (c) genuine absence definitively ruled out
ID.reasoning: A mature, decades-deep Indonesian-language body of formal standards exists (SNI 03-1735-2000 since 2000; Kepmen PU 468/KPTS/1998; Permen PUPR 14/2017) plus current academic literature including a wayfinding-for-visually-disabled-students paper that matches a corpus topic slug almost verbatim -- all surfaced on the first reasonably-phrased Indonesian query.

HI.verdict: (b) query construction -- (a) tooling reachability ruled out
HI.reasoning: Native-Devanagari-script queries immediately surfaced India's Harmonised Guidelines and Space Standards for Barrier-Free Built Environment (MoHUA), including what appears to be an official Hindi translation. Romanized/naive-transliterated queries returned only English content -- a clean demonstration of the failure mode.

recovered_not_ingested.title: Hindi translation of the Harmonised Guidelines and Space Standards for Barrier-Free Built Environment
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch (filename/title only); PDF not directly opened (403); needs independent verification before ingest

BN.verdict: mixed, leaning (b) query construction (subtler -- wrong terminology, not just wrong script)
BN.reasoning: The naive-term native-script query underperformed; switching to the Bangladesh-specific technical term "সার্বজনীনগম্য" (universal-accessibility) surfaced real content: BNBC 2020 Part 3 Appendix D (Universal Accessibility), the 2013 Rights and Protection of Persons with Disabilities Act's accessibility clause, and a dedicated Bengali-language guideline site (BUAG).

recovered_not_ingested.title: BUAG -- Bengali-language accessible-building guideline site
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch; not directly read (proxy-blocked); publishing org not confirmed; flagged

recovered_not_ingested.title: Rights and Protection of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2013 (accessibility clause, ~Sec. 34)
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch; not directly read; flagged

AR.verdict: (b) query construction -- (c) genuine absence firmly ruled out
AR.reasoning: Properly-phrased Modern Standard Arabic queries on niche sub-topics (wayfinding for the blind, classroom acoustics, dementia sensory design) all returned substantive relevant results immediately; a naive/garbled-transliteration query on the identical topic returned nothing useful -- a clean side-by-side demonstration. Real, current, binding national codes exist: Saudi SBC 201/901, Dubai Universal Design Code, Egyptian Code 601 (under Law 10/2018).

global_south_zero_result_investigation.tooling_caveat: Multiple independent investigating agents in batch 1 reported that direct WebFetch was blocked (HTTP 403) on a broad, seemingly unrelated set of government/NGO/standards-body domains across ALL FIVE languages' investigations, confirmed via the environment's own proxy-status endpoint as a shared infrastructure issue that session, not source-specific unavailability. This is flagged as a separate corpus-wide risk: if the ORIGINAL 81x19 multilingual sweep also depended on WebFetch (not just search) for a similar domain set, some fraction of ALL 'zero hits' (not just the 5 flagged languages) could be a tooling artifact rather than a genuine absence. Worth a dedicated audit.

batch2_direct_read_reattempt.summary: Batch 2 made a SECOND, independent attempt to directly open and read (not just WebSearch-corroborate) all 9 recovered-not-ingested candidates from batch 1 (3 Indonesian, 1 Hindi, 3 Bangladeshi, 2 of the 4 Arabic-region items -- Saudi SBC201 and Egyptian Code 601 re-attempted; Dubai/IDA not re-attempted). Result: 0/9 achieved a genuine direct read.
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.root_cause_now_precisely_diagnosed: Unlike batch 1's framing (a plausible shared-proxy issue), batch 2's investigating agents ran CONTROL-URL TESTS (fetching https://example.com, https://en.wikipedia.org, https://www.anthropic.com) and found these ALSO returned HTTP 403, and Bash curl's CONNECT tunnel was rejected for nearly every host tried. This confirms a SESSION-WIDE WebFetch tooling outage this session, not target-site blocking, not a domain-allowlist issue, and not evidence about whether the underlying sources are real (WebSearch, which uses a different retrieval path, continued to work and is the sole basis for the corroboration below).
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.one_new_finding: The Indonesian-search agent flagged a real discrepancy needing resolution before citing: SNI 03-1735-2000's actual title per fresh search snippets appears to include a fire-hazard-prevention qualifier ('...untuk Pencegahan Bahaya Kebakaran Pada Bangunan Gedung') not present in the original candidate description -- this may mean the standard's primary scope is fire-egress access, not general disability accessibility. Needs a direct read to resolve, not assumed either way.
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.recommendation: Do not attempt a third WebSearch-only verification pass on these 9 candidates -- the blocker is tooling (WebFetch outage), not insufficient searching. Retry only from a session with confirmed-functional WebFetch (verify against a trivial control URL first).
